---
title: "Unidad 10.4: Hardening y Seguridad en Nginx"
subtitle: "Headers, rate limiting y configuración segura"
author: "Diego Saavedra"
date: "2026-03-31"
date-format: medium
---

# Unidad 10.4: Hardening y Seguridad en Nginx

## Introducción

Hardening de Nginx significa: ocultar información del servidor, agregar headers de seguridad, configurar rate limiting, y proteger recursos sensibles.

## Hardening Básico

### 1. Ocultar Información del Servidor

```nginx
# En http block de nginx.conf
server_tokens off;  # <1>

# En server block
more_clear_headers Server;  # <2>
```

1. **server_tokens off**: Hides the nginx version from response headers — only shows "nginx" instead of "nginx/1.24.0".
2. **more_clear_headers Server**: Removes the Server header entirely — requires Headers More module.

**Antes vs Después:**

```bash
# Antes
$ curl -I http://localhost | grep Server
Server: nginx/1.24.0 (Ubuntu)

# Después
$ curl -I http://localhost | grep Server
Server: nginx
# <1>
```

1. **curl -I**: Shows only response headers — confirms version is hidden after applying `server_tokens off`.

### 2. Limitar Información en Headers

```nginx
# En nginx.conf - http block
client_max_body_size 10M;        # <1>
client_body_timeout 60s;          # <2>
client_header_buffer_size 1k;    # <3>
large_client_header_buffers 4 16k;  # <4>
```

1. **client_max_body_size 10M**: Limits request body size to 10MB — returns 413 if exceeded.
2. **client_body_timeout 60s**: Closes slow request bodies after 60 seconds — prevents slowloris attacks.
3. **client_header_buffer_size 1k**: Allocates 1KB for each request header line — enough for most headers.
4. **large_client_header_buffers 4 16k**: Up to 4 buffers of 16KB for large headers — handles long cookies or URLs.

### 3. Protección de Archivos

```nginx
# Bloquear archivos sensibles
location ~ /\. {
    deny all;  # <1>
    access_log off;
    log_not_found off;
}

# Archivos específicos
location ~* \.(htaccess|htpasswd|git|env|config|ini|xml|json|md|yml)$ {
    deny all;  # <2>
}
```

1. **deny all**: Blocks access to hidden files like `.htaccess`, `.git`, `.env` — returns 403 for any path starting with `.`.
2. **deny all**: Blocks config and metadata files — prevents exposure of credentials, git history, and internal data.

## Security Headers

### Headers Esenciales

```bash
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/snippets/security-headers.conf <<'EOF'
# X-Content-Type-Options
add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;  # <1>

# X-Frame-Options
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;  # <2>

# Referrer-Policy
add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" always;  # <3>

# X-XSS-Protection
add_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block" always;  # <4>

# Permissions-Policy
add_header Permissions-Policy "geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()" always;  # <5>

# Strict-Transport-Security (solo con SSL)
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" always;  # <6>
EOF
```

1. **nosniff**: Prevents MIME type sniffing — browser won't guess content type from bytes.
2. **SAMEORIGIN**: Blocks iframe embedding from other domains — prevents clickjacking.
3. **strict-origin-when-cross-origin**: Sends origin on cross-origin requests, full URL on same-origin — balances privacy and referrer utility.
4. **1; mode=block**: Enables browser XSS filter and blocks suspicious content instead of sanitizing.
5. **Permissions-Policy**: Disables geolocation, microphone, camera APIs — prevents unauthorized device access.
6. **max-age=63072000**: HSTS for 2 years — browser always uses HTTPS for this domain.

### Incluir en Server Block

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name example.com;
    
    # Security headers (solo funciona con SSL para HSTS)
    include /etc/nginx/snippets/security-headers.conf;  # <1>
    
    # Resto de configuración...
}
```

1. **include snippets**: Loads the security headers file — keeps config DRY across multiple server blocks.

### Verificar Headers

```bash
# Ver todos los headers
$ curl -I http://localhost
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Server: nginx
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN
Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block
Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()
# <1>
```

1. **curl -I**: Shows all response headers — confirms all security headers are applied correctly.

### Content Security Policy (CSP)

```bash
# CSP restrictiva - ajustar según aplicación
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/snippets/csp.conf <<'EOF'
# Basic CSP
add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https:; font-src 'self' data:;" always;
EOF

# CSP más permisiva (para desarrollo)
add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'; img-src 'self' data: https: *; connect-src 'self' https: wss:;" always;  # <1>
```

1. **unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval**: Permits inline scripts and eval — acceptable in dev, dangerous in production.

## Rate Limiting

### Configuración Básica

```nginx
# En http block
http {
    # Definir zone de rate limiting
    limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=general:10M rate=10r/s;  # <1>
    limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=api:10M rate=100r/s;  # <2>
}
```

1. **$binary_remote_addr zone=general:10M rate=10r/s**: Creates a 10MB rate limiting zone keyed by client IP — limits general traffic to 10 requests/second.
2. **zone=api:10M rate=100r/s**: Separate zone for API with higher limit — allows 100 requests/second for API endpoints.

### Aplicar Rate Limit

```nginx
# Rate limit general (10 req/segundo, burst de 20)
location / {
    limit_req zone=general burst=20 nodelay;  # <1>
}

# Rate limit para API (100 req/segundo)
location /api/ {
    limit_req zone=api burst=50 nodelay;  # <2>
}
```

1. **burst=20 nodelay**: Allows burst of 20 requests without delay — handles legitimate traffic spikes.
2. **burst=50 nodelay**: Higher burst for API — accommodates API call patterns.

### Rate Limit con Whitelist

```bash
# Excluir IPs conocidas
http {
    geo $limited {
        default 1;  # <1>
        10.0.0.0/8 0;  # <2>
        172.16.0.0/12 0;
        192.168.0.0/16 0;
    }
    
    limit_req_zone $limited zone=general:10M rate=10r/s;  # <3>
}
```

1. **default 1**: All IPs are limited by default — opt-out model.
2. **0**: Private IPs bypass rate limiting — internal traffic isn't throttled.
3. **$limited**: Uses geo variable instead of raw IP — whitelisted IPs get value 0 (not tracked).

### Verificar Rate Limiting

```bash
# Test con curl rápido
$ for i in {1..15}; do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://localhost/api/; done
# <1>

# Ver logs de rate limit
$ sudo tail /var/log/nginx/error.log
2026/03/31 10:15:00 [warn] 1234#1234: *1001 limiting connections by zone "general"
# <2>
```

1. **for loop**: Sends 15 rapid requests — after burst is exhausted, returns 429.
2. **tail error.log**: Shows rate limit warnings — confirms the zone is active and blocking.

## Conexiones y Tiempo

### Configuración de Keep-Alive

```nginx
# En http block
keepalive_timeout 65;  # <1>
keepalive_requests 100;

# En server block
keepalive_timeout 30s;  # <2>
keepalive_requests 50;
```

1. **65 seconds**: Default keepalive timeout — connection closes after 65s of inactivity.
2. **30s override**: Server block value overrides http block — shorter timeout for this server.

### Limits de Conexiones

```nginx
# Limitar conexiones por IP
limit_conn_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=addr:10M;  # <1>

server {
    location / {
        limit_conn addr 10;  # <2>
    }
}
```

1. **zone=addr:10M**: Creates connection limiting zone — tracks concurrent connections per IP.
2. **limit_conn addr 10**: Max 10 concurrent connections per IP — prevents connection exhaustion.

## Protección de Backend

### Proxy con Autenticación

```nginx
# Autenticación básica al backend
location / {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
    proxy_set_header Authorization $http_authorization;  # <1>
    
    allow 10.0.0.0/8;  # <2>
    allow 172.16.0.0/12;
    allow 192.168.0.0/16;
    deny all;  # <3>
}
```

1. **proxy_set_header Authorization**: Forwards auth header to backend — preserves client credentials.
2. **allow 10.0.0.0/8**: Only private networks can reach backend — blocks external access.
3. **deny all**: Catches everything else — explicit deny after allows is a whitelist pattern.

### Buffering de Respuestas

```nginx
# Configuración de buffering
proxy_buffering on;  # <1>
proxy_buffer_size 4k;  # <2>
proxy_buffers 8 4k;  # <3>
proxy_busy_buffers_size 8k;  # <4>
proxy_max_temp_file_size 1024m;  # <5>
```

1. **proxy_buffering on**: Enables response buffering — client receives data faster.
2. **proxy_buffer_size 4k**: First buffer for response headers — enough for most responses.
3. **proxy_buffers 8 4k**: 8 buffers of 4KB each for body — total 32KB before disk spill.
4. **proxy_busy_buffers_size 8k**: Can send 8KB to client while still buffering — improves throughput.
5. **proxy_max_temp_file_size 1024m**: Max temp file for large responses — prevents disk exhaustion.

## Hardening Completo de Producción

### Configuración Completa

```bash
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/production-secure.conf <<'EOF'
server {
    listen 80;
    listen [::]:80;
    server_name example.com www.example.com;
    
    # Security headers (no SSL aún)
    include /etc/nginx/snippets/security-headers.conf;
    
    # Rate limiting
    limit_req zone=general burst=20 nodelay;
    
    root /var/www/example.com/public;
    index index.html index.htm;
    
    # Proteger archivos
    location ~ /\. {
        deny all;
    }
    
    location ~* \.(htaccess|htpasswd|git|env|config|ini|xml|json|md|yml)$ {
        deny all;
    }
    
    # Static files con cache
    location ~* \.(jpg|jpeg|png|gif|ico|css|js|woff|woff2|ttf|svg)$ {
        expires 30d;
        add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
        access_log off;
    }
    
    # Logs
    access_log /var/log/nginx/example.com-access.log;
    error_log /var/log/nginx/example.com-error.log;
}

# HTTPS server block
server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    listen [::]:443 ssl http2;
    server_name example.com www.example.com;
    
    # SSL
    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/privkey.pem;
    include /etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-nginx.conf;
    
    # Security headers con HSTS
    include /etc/nginx/snippets/security-headers.conf;
    
    # Rate limiting
    limit_req zone=general burst=20 nodelay;
    
    root /var/www/example.com/public;
    index index.html index.htm;
    
    # Proteger archivos
    location ~ /\. {
        deny all;
    }
    
    # PHP processing
    location ~ \.php$ {
        deny all;
    }
    
    # Logs
    access_log /var/log/nginx/example.com-ssl-access.log;
    error_log /var/log/nginx/example.com-ssl-error.log;
}

# Redirect HTTP to HTTPS
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name example.com www.example.com;
    return 301 https://$server_name$request_uri;
}
EOF
# <1>
```

1. **Configuración producción**: Bloque HTTP con security headers + rate limiting + protección de archivos + cache estático. Bloque HTTPS agrega SSL + HSTS. Bloque de redirect fuerza HTTPS para todo el tráfico.

## Casos de Estudio

### Caso 1: Servidor Recibiendo Ataques

**Síntomas**:
```bash
# Miles de requests desde una IP
$ sudo tail /var/log/nginx/access.log | awk '{print $1}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head
   5000 45.155.205.233
    500 10.0.0.50
# <1>
```

**Solución**:
```bash
# 1. Rate limiting
$ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx

# 2. Bloquear IP específica
$ sudo ufw insert 1 deny from 45.155.205.233

# 3. Verificar
$ sudo tail /var/log/nginx/error.log
# <2>
```

1. **sort -nr | head**: Sorts by count descending — quickly identifies the most aggressive IP.

### Caso 2: Headers No Aparecen

**Problema**:
```bash
$ curl -I http://localhost | grep -i X-Frame
# (sin output = headers no aplicados)
# <1>
```

**Diagnóstico**:
```bash
# Ver si el include está en la configuración
$ grep -r "security-headers" /etc/nginx/

# Verificar sintaxis
$ sudo nginx -t
# <2>
```

1. **Headers missing**: No se están aplicando

**Solución**:
```bash
# Verificar que el archivo existe
$ ls -la /etc/nginx/snippets/security-headers.conf  # <1>

# Agregar manualmente al server block
location / {
    add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;  # <2>
    # ...
}
```

1. **ls -la**: Confirms the snippet file exists — if missing, the include directive silently fails.
2. **add_header inline**: Fallback when snippet include doesn't work — manually adds the header.

### Caso 3: Upload de Archivos Grande

**Problema**:
```bash
$ curl -X POST -F "file=@large.zip" https://example.com/upload
413 Request Entity Too Large
# <1>
```

**Solución**:
```nginx
# Aumentar límite
client_max_body_size 100M;  # <1>
proxy_buffering off;  # <2>

location /upload {
    client_max_body_size 100M;  # <3>
}
```

1. **100M**: Increases limit globally — allows large file uploads.
2. **proxy_buffering off**: Disables buffering for proxied responses — streams data directly to client.
3. **location override**: Per-path limit — only `/upload` accepts 100MB, rest stays at default.

1. **413**: Body muy grande

## Checklist de Seguridad Nginx

| Configuración | Valor | Estado |
|---------------|-------|--------|
| server_tokens | off | ⏳ |
| X-Frame-Options | SAMEORIGIN | ⏳ |
| X-Content-Type-Options | nosniff | ⏳ |
| Referrer-Policy | strict-origin-when-cross-origin | ⏳ |
| HSTS | max-age=63072000 | ⏳ |
| Rate Limit | 10-100 rps | ⏳ |
| client_max_body_size | 10M | ⏳ |
| SSL Protocols | TLSv1.2+ | ⏳ |

## Resumen

| Tema | Comando Clave | Propósito |
|------|---------------|-----------|
| Server Tokens | `server_tokens off` | Ocultar versión |
| Headers | `add_header X-*` | Seguridad HTTP |
| Rate Limit | `limit_req_zone` | Prevenir DoS |
| Files | `deny all` | Bloquear acceso |
| SSL | `ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3` | TLS seguro |

## Referencias

- [Nginx Security Headers](https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_headers_module.html)
- [Nginx Rate Limiting](https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_limit_req_module.html)
- [OWASP Security Headers](https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/HTTP_Security_Headers_Cheat_Sheet.html)
